D8 AnthropologyL3 ApplyReference

Malachite & Azurite — Same Copper, Different Crystal

Cu₂CO₃(OH)₂ malachite (green) and Cu₃(CO₃)₂(OH)₂ azurite (blue) differ only in stoichiometry; azurite slowly weathers to malachite, fading 15th-century skies to leaf-green

Both pigments are basic copper carbonates ground from mined ore. Cu²⁺ d-d transitions give green for malachite, blue for azurite — the difference is the surrounding ligand geometry. In humid air azurite slowly converts to the more stable malachite; medieval paintings of the Virgin's robe often have turned green where the sky-blue once was.

Slide weathering: 0 = fresh azurite, 1 = fully converted malachite.
